channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Missouri man facing execution in the sexual assault and strangling of a 9-year-old girl
item.description: BONNE TERRE, Mo. (AP) — A Missouri man is scheduled to be put to death Tuesday evening for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl whose body was thrown into a sinkhole. Christopher Collings, 49, was set to receive a single injection of the sedative pentobarbital at or after 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California man loses $6,000 in scam targeting grandparents
item.description: The scammer posed as the man’s grandson and used “fear-based tactics and a false sense of urgency” to make off with the victim’s cash.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel luring chemistry students to make fentanyl: Report
item.description: Recruitment of chemistry students at Mexican universities is part of the Sinaloa cartel's strategy to expand its fentanyl empire.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Safety patrols receive threats after entire police force resigns, mayor's death
item.description: Volunteer safety teams said they received a telephone threat warning to "Stay out of McColl" and that it should "Mind its own business.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: US biathletes fear retaliation for speaking out about sexual harassment, report finds
item.description: U.S. biathletes face “misogynistic” behavior while racing and fear retaliation if they report their concerns, according to an inquiry by an independent contractor hired to assess athlete safety. The investigation of U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Don't miss these holiday shipping deadlines
item.description: The peak holiday shopping – and shipping – season is underway. If you want to make sure your gifts make it under the tree in time, you'll need to plan ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These are the worst cities for package theft in 2024
item.description: The holiday season, which has already seen a surge in online shopping, presents even more opportunities for package thieves.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service said Monday nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week for this year's presidential contest, despite hurricanes, some misdirected election mail and delivery concerns raised by state officials.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: An ex-detective accused of abusing women died in an apparent suicide as his trial was starting
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A white ex-police detective in Kansas died Monday in an apparent suicide just before the start of his criminal trial over allegations that he sexually assaulted Black women and terrorized those who tried fight back.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and NICK INGRAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Prosecutor says officers acted 'recklessly' in Black man's death during mental health crisis
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers charged in the death of a Black man — who was shocked with a Taser during a mental health crisis — acted “recklessly" by restraining him face down longer than necessary, a prosecutor said during opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio schools offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: MARYSVILLE, Ohio (WCMH) -- A new religious release program for public school students is entering Marysville schools, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Best Cyber Monday 2023 tablet deals that make great gifts
item.description: BestReviews is reader-supported and may earn an affiliate commission. Details. Score deals on tablets from Apple, Samsung and Amazon Cyber Monday is here, and one constant is that you can find major savings on tech like tablets, both online and in stores.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Luna, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UN halts aid shipments through Gaza's main crossing after looting. It blames the crisis on Israel
item.description: The U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Sunday it is halting aid deliveries through the main cargo crossing into the war-ravaged Gaza Strip because of the threat of armed gangs who have looted convoys. It blamed the breakdown of law and order in large part on Israeli policies.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, KTLA 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: New Starbucks gives customers view of a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Customers have to pass a military checkpoint before entering the observatory where the new Starbucks has opened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lou Carnesecca, Hall of Fame coach who led St. John's for 24 seasons, dies
item.description: The excitable St. John’s coach whose outlandish sweaters became an emblem of his team’s rousing Final Four run in 1985, died at 99 on Saturday, just a few weeks shy of his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in national park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, ABC 4

item.title: Hero Girl Scout saves dad after he suffered heart attack in Virginia
item.description: Vada Carawan's Virginia Beach Girl Scout troop had just learned CPR 11 days earlier, and in that moment last Saturday, with her father unresponsive and her dog upset, she channeled her energies into saving him.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau flew to Florida on Friday to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WGN 9

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives. For one family in Rockland County, this is a special holiday. A daughter and her biological father are together.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, WGN 9

item.title: Woman to reunite with brother missing for 25 years after spotting his picture in news
item.description: (KTLA) - A man missing since 1999 has been identified and is set to reunite with his family after media coverage helped bring the decades-long case to a close.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time: Black Friday, the bargain bonanza that still reigns as the unofficial kickoff of the holiday shopping season even if it's lost some luster
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9
